accept: HLC.Less or its callers (apply.go LWW path) tiebreak by op_hash, matching the spec and claimLess.
If HLC values don't carry op_hash directly, the comparison surface grows to take both an HLC and an op_hash; or LWW is reframed as (HLC, op_hash) tuples.
Property test: two ops with identical (wall, logical, hash) but different nodes resolve consistently across LWW and claim paths.
Spec-v2.md unchanged; this is a code fix, not a spec fix.
payload.description:
Found by code reviewer 2026-05-10 at internal/hlc/hlc.go:51-59. HLC.Less breaks (wall, logical) ties by NodeID. Spec spec-v2.md:347 and claim/claim.go:420-428 (claimLess) tiebreak by op_hash (full SHA-256). HLC.Less is used for LWW field comparison in apply.go:34-39; claimLess is used for claim winner selection. So two ops with identical (wall, logical) from different nodes resolve by NodeID for LWW field merges and by op_hash for claim winners — divergent rules in different code paths. Real correctness concern in multi-writer scenarios where (wall, logical) collide.

Spec is the source of truth: tiebreak by op_hash everywhere.
payload.title: HLC.Less uses NodeID; op-fold tiebreaks by op_hash
